name: Delicioso Asopao DE Gandules
intro: Asopao de gandules is a soup made with pigeon peas. It is flavored with herbs and spices and aromatics; green plantain bowls and rice are added, making it fulfilling and a warm bowl of love. The best way to form the plantain balls is with a grater or a blender, so that when you drop them into the soup, they do not break up. Also, leave this step for last, after everything is cooked.

ingredients.item: vegan vegetable bouillon cube, or 1 tbsp (18 g) vegetable bouillon paste
package frozen pigeon peas, or 2 (15-oz [425-g]) cans, drained and rinsed
uncooked medium-grain rice, washed and soaked for 1 hour
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
calabaza squash, seeded and cut into 6 pieces

method: In a large soup pot over medium heat, combine your oil, garlic paste, sofrito, vegetable bouillon and onion. Sauté to release the aromatics and soften the onion.
Add the tomato sauce, Sazón, pigeon peas and vegetable stock. Cook the soup, stirring occasionally. When the peas start to soften, add your rice, olive mixture, culantro, cilantro and oregano.
While the soup cooks, peel your plantains. You can either grate them or place them in a blender, then lightly season with salt and pepper. Shape the grated plantains into six to eight small balls.
Check back on the soup. When the rice has softened and doubled in size, add in the calabaza and cook for an additional 6 minutes, or until the calabaza is tender. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Next, gently drop the plantain balls into the soup to cook. Allow the soup to simmer, covered, over low heat for about 15 minutes while stirring occasionally, then serve hot.
